steel.alternative: Recycled Steel / Electric Arc Furnace (EAF) Steel
steel.implementation: Source steel from EAF producers using recycled scrap. EAF steel produces ~0.4 tCO2/t vs ~1.85 tCO2/t for BF-BOF. Verify supplier EPDs and request carbon intensity certificates.

aluminum.implementation: Switch to recycled aluminum which requires only 5% of the energy of primary production. Source from certified recyclers. Recycled aluminum emits ~0.5 tCO2/t vs ~16.7 tCO2/t for primary.

cement.implementation: Replace OPC with PPC (50% clinker reduction) or geopolymer cement like E-Crete which uses industrial by-products. Conduct structural testing for your application. PPC emits ~0.35 tCO2/t vs ~0.89 tCO2/t for OPC.

concrete.implementation: Use geopolymer concrete with fly ash or slag as binder instead of OPC. Reduces embodied carbon by 50-80%. Test compressive strength for structural compliance.

glass.implementation: Increase recycled cullet content to 80%+. Each 10% increase in cullet reduces energy by 2.5%. Source from certified glass recyclers.

limestone.alternative: Industrial By-product Calcium Sources / Recycled calcium carbonate
limestone.implementation: Replace virgin limestone with calcium-rich industrial by-products (steel slag, waste calcium carbonate). Reduces mining emissions and calcination CO2.

natural gas.implementation: Replace natural gas with biogas from anaerobic digestion or green hydrogen from electrolysis. Start with a pilot burner, then scale. Biomethane is a drop-in replacement for existing gas infrastructure.

coal.implementation: Replace coal with biomass pellets or RDF in kilns/boilers. Biomass is considered carbon-neutral under GHG Protocol. Requires burner modifications. BioChar technology can convert agricultural waste into green fuel briquettes.

diesel.implementation: Switch fleet and generators to B100 biodiesel or Hydrotreated Vegetable Oil (HVO). For transport, evaluate electric vehicle alternatives. HVO is a drop-in replacement requiring no engine modifications.
